title: Merge a Divergent Branch
description:
## Integrate a Feature With a Merge Commit

The repository at **`/root/mergerepo`** has diverged: `main` and `feature` each have commits the other lacks (`feature` added **`feature.txt`**). You are on `main`.

**Your task:** merge `feature` into `main` so that:
1. `main` contains **`feature.txt`**
2. the merge creates a **merge commit** (use `--no-ff` so a merge commit is recorded even if a fast-forward were possible)

> 💡 `git merge --no-ff feature -m "merge feature"`. Because the branches diverged, a merge commit with two parents is produced.

hints.title: Create a merge commit
hints.body: --no-ff forces a merge commit; -m supplies its message so no editor opens.

setup_commands.command: cd /root/mergerepo && echo base > base.txt && git add base.txt && git commit -m base
cd /root/mergerepo && git switch -c feature && echo feat > feature.txt && git add feature.txt && git commit -m feature
cd /root/mergerepo && git switch main && echo more >> base.txt && git commit -am 'main work'

validation.description: Checks that main contains feature.txt and history has exactly one merge commit.
